reason: This task PRD — 5 ADR-lite (Q0-Q5), R1-R26 requirements, AC-1~AC-25, 3 PR breakdown, R-Risk-1~5
executeReport / executeAnalyze are new inner functions sharing RunCtx with existing executeXxx — single-point dispatchStep router invariant must hold (W2-1 PR3 pattern)
WorkflowStage + FlowId added in shared; consumed by runner orchestrator + FLOW_REGISTRY; KNOWN_FLOW_IDS dual list lives in api route + runner index — alignment across shared/runner/api is critical
Canonical FLOW_REGISTRY contracts (W2-3 updated) — § 5.5 'Adding a new flow' recipe is the literal checklist for W2-2a; spec append section to extend
W2-1 ADR Q1=α (kind/skillId placeholder), Q2 (flow_id NOT NULL DEFAULT), Q3 (createWorkflowRunInput.flowId? entry), Q4 (zero-regression test) — W2-2a inherits all four
W2-3 thin extension pattern reference (~3 PR / 1 week cadence); R-Risk-2 (fastforward acceptance gate) is the open issue Q3 stage-history-aware gate-engine refactor will fix as side effect
Wave 2 roadmap — Q4 naming convention (FlowId / StageStep / KnowledgeArtifactKind shared constraints); W2-2 split into W2-2a/W2-2b is Q0 ADR extension
V2 design source — § 1.3 V1 process rigidity / § 2.1 philosophy 1 work-type polymorphism / § 4.2 phase 1 three workflows (issue is the second) / § 6 design-trap 4 (covering 100% = none deep)
WorkflowStage / FlowId / WorkflowRun.flowId in packages/shared/src/types/workflow.ts; shared types enum no narrowing + literal union extension are core of W2-2a PR1
TS quality bar — FlowId must be string-literal union (not free string); new enum values need JSDoc; FLOW_REGISTRY's Readonly<Record> guards exhaustiveness

runner backend spec index — orchestrator main + dispatchStep + executeXxx inner-function style; SkillSpec/findSkillForStage in apps/runner/src/skills/index.ts; KNOWN_FLOW_IDS validation in apps/runner/src/index.ts
runner quality bar — _exhaustive: never guards dispatchStep switch; RunCtx not exported; SkillSpec instructions are contract layer not impl detail
executeReport / executeAnalyze failure paths — `[runner] orchestration failed` catch in cmdOrchestrate top-level; c.ok.value = false rather than swallowing throw is the contract locked by W2-1 PR3
api backend spec index — gate-engine in apps/api/src/gate-engine.ts; runAcceptanceTraceabilityGate at line 454-504; routes/workflow-runs.ts holds second KNOWN_FLOW_IDS list
api quality bar — gate-engine single responsibility (no FlowId / FLOW_REGISTRY knowledge); RuleResult status='pass' with N/A note is minimal change for Q3 stage-history-aware path
stepRuns.byWorkflow(workflowRunId) is the existing store query Q3 stage-history-aware uses; no schema change in this task
